notes: Strong stylistic fit overall, but only one obvious 95-108 BPM candidate on the first 150-track page.

Useful taste anchor, but no usable 95-108 BPM tracks surfaced on the first 150-track page.
Productive in the 95-108 BPM band, but requires curation because the catalog ranges from shadowy psychill to brighter cosmic material.
No usable 95-108 BPM tracks surfaced on the first 150-track page.

Strong third-pass branch with a dense 95-108 BPM pocket and several darker minor-key electronica cuts.
Does produce low-BPM material, but the overall tone leans more organic/ritual/uplifting than shadowy-seductive.
Selective yes: a real dark low-BPM neighbor, but noisier than Mystic with significant bass-house, dubstep, and breakbeat spillover on the first 150-track page.
Guy J's label; discovered via People Also Bought on Aphelion (Noom UK). Guy J is a top-tier dark progressive house artist. HIGH PRIORITY for 108-122 BPM pass.
Nick Warren's label; discovered via People Also Bought on Aphelion (Noom UK). Nick Warren is a seminal dark progressive figure. HIGH PRIORITY.
Paul Hazendonk's label; discovered via People Also Bought on Aphelion (Noom UK). Progressive house with potential dark pocket.
Xspance + Daniela Rhodes release spotted via People Also Bought on Aphelion.
Premium dark progressive house label. Nicolas Rada + Armen Miran collab (Corner of the Room EP). Fall Away and Pull both added to cart. HIGH PRIORITY for further exploration.
Appears 4+ times in recommendations: Hraach, Armen Miran, Eli Nissan, Pambouk, Hrag Mikkel, Serious Dancers. Organic House dominant genre but has dark material. Worth selective exploration.
DAVI, Hraach, Armen Miran collaboration (Boarding Call). Dark progressive crossover potential.
Nick Warren + Nicolas Rada release (Buenos Aires). Nick Warren's label for deeper material.
Nick Warren's compilation/label brand. Armen Miran appears (Another Green Mile).
Nikola Jovanovic label. Appears twice in People Also Bought. Dark progressive potential.
Progressive house label. Redemption Sound release spotted.
Strong progressive house label. Shelter of Hearts by GMJ & Matter added to cart. Alex O'Rion also releases here. HIGH PRIORITY.
Dark progressive house label. The Serpent (Imran Khan Remix) by Alex O'Rion added to cart. Kasper Koman also releases here (Biome, Sinking Sky). HIGH PRIORITY for further exploration.
foglight's Progressive House label. Gridlock (122 BPM, F Minor) is a candidate for investigation.
Progressive house label. Endless Love (HAFT Remix) by Wassu (120 BPM, E Minor) is a candidate.
Organic House label appearing frequently in recommendations. Multiple minor-key tracks but genre leans organic. Selective exploration only.
GMJ & Matter release (Metanoia). Progressive house label.
Imran Khan releases here (Hagel, Mystic Sea). Established progressive house label.
Jamie Stevens + Alex O'Rion collaboration label. With You (Alex O'Rion Remix) at 122 BPM B Minor is a strong candidate. Jamie Stevens is a confirmed productive artist from Session 1.
Hernan Cattaneo's label. Alex O'Rion's Atlas (122 BPM D Minor) is a strong candidate. HIGH PRIORITY — Hernan Cattaneo is a legendary dark progressive DJ.
Alex O'Rion + Vinson release (Circular Progression). Progressive house label.
Juan Deminicis label. Going Nowhere (120 BPM F Minor) added to cart. Progressive house label.
Fran Garay releases here (Cavernas, 120 BPM G Minor). Cross-reference with Solis Records where Fran Garay is also productive.
Gorkiz releases here (Flusterstorm, 122 BPM G Minor). New dark progressive label.
GMJ & Matter remix of Oceans in Between (120 BPM D Minor). Appeared on multiple recommendation surfaces. Progressive house label.
Sensus (Matter Remix) by Robert R. Hardy (121 BPM Eb Minor). Progressive house label.
Dream One by Amháin (122 BPM E Minor). Progressive house label.
Inner Growth by Tomas Harguintegui (120 BPM F Minor). Progressive house label.

Deep house label. Armen Miran's Heavenly Life (118 BPM Db Minor) added. Worth selective exploration for deeper material.
Kasper Koman releases here. Wilder (120 BPM D Minor) and In Circles (121 BPM C Minor) added. Progressive house label.

notes: JOOF Aura yielded 5 stylistically strong cart adds in the 115-120 BPM zone from the prior pass.
This pass is prioritizing lower BPM discoveries first, so neighboring labels are now higher priority than deeper JOOF Aura mining.
Fresh snapshot confirmation showed the following low-BPM additions in cart: Kinetic Flow, Dischord, Thauma, Night Vision, Soldier's Daughter.
Beatport's 'People Also Bought' / 'Recommendations' on Night Vision drifted into broad electronica and was less useful than artist-overlap graph expansion.
Cart count reached 15 by the end of this pass.
Third pass extended beyond the initial one-hour target and treated that window as a floor rather than a stop.
People Also Bought / Recommendations on Soldier's Daughter also drifted toward generic electronica, reinforcing that artist-overlap graphing is the stronger discovery tactic here.
Third-pass clean adds from Mystic Sound Records: Dream Engine, Blurry Horizon.
Third-pass Beatport duplicate protection excluded Nuage d'Encens and Secret Space as previously purchased.
Cart count reached 17 during the third pass.
Clock-time continuation restarted at 2026-04-13T22:04:41Z and is being treated as a true wall-clock floor for active exploration.
Unfinished Monkey Records produced two strong adds (Secret of the Logos, Midnight) and should remain an active but selective neighbor.
LW Recordings appears to be a weak overlap branch for this brief and should stay deprioritized.
People Also Bought on Midnight drifted into bass-heavy UK-bass / dubstep territory and was materially less useful than artist-led or label-graph exploration.
Dreaming Cooper inside Mystic Sound Records surfaced Wide Spectrum as another strong fit.
Cart count reached 20 during the clock-time continuation.

A separate 108-122 BPM pass used 150 results per page and crawled up to 10 pages on promising labels before narrowing to JOOF Aura, Bedrock Records, Stripped Recordings, and artist-led Verche expansion.

10-page label census showed the biggest raw volume in-range on Visceral, Particles, and Stripped Recordings, but JOOF Aura stayed cleaner and more on-brief while Bedrock strengthened materially versus the low-BPM pass.
Artist-depth scan confirmed Verche as a productive cross-label node in this band, surfacing Hades, Healer, and Night at the Velvet Mountain.
108-122 BPM clean adds in the current Chrome DevTools cart context: Vanaheim, Embrace Me, Zero Dark, Miragem, The Dying Light, Hades, Healer, Night at the Velvet Mountain.
Current Chrome DevTools Beatport cart count reached 8 during the 108-122 BPM pass.
Session 2 started at 2026-04-15T15:30:19Z with 108-122 BPM focus and 60-120 minute runtime bounds.

Noom (UK) artist page crawl surfaced Aphelion (119 BPM, D Minor) as a strong add. People Also Bought on Aphelion was highly productive: Guy J (Lost & Found), Nick Warren (onedotsixtwo), Paul Hazendonk (ICONYC).
Cart count reached 10 after Aphelion add in Session 2.
Lost & Found label scan via Levantine (122 BPM, G Minor) surfaced a rich recommendation graph: Freedom Fighters, Khen, Kasper Koman, Nicolas Rada, Armen Miran.
People Also Bought on Levantine was the single most productive recommendation scan in Session 2: led to Balance Music, Hoomidaas, Moonlight, Future Avenue, and multiple strong artist nodes.
Balance Music emerged as a top-tier label for this brief: Armen Miran + Nicolas Rada collab EP (Corner of the Room) yielded Fall Away and Pull.
Recommendations on Fall Away cross-validated with Levantine: Loco Motif Tantum Remix appeared on both, confirming the Lost & Found → Balance Music corridor as the strongest graph neighborhood.
Solis Records emerged via recommendations: Shelter of Hearts by GMJ & Matter (121 BPM E Minor) — strong dark progressive fit.
Meanwhile emerged as a high-value label: The Serpent (Imran Khan Remix) by Alex O'Rion (121 BPM Bb Minor) added. Kasper Koman also releases on Meanwhile (Biome, Sinking Sky).
Session 2 added 7 tracks: Levantine, Fall Away, Pull, Loco Motif (Tantum Remix), Shelter of Hearts, The Serpent (Imran Khan Remix). Aphelion was added earlier in Session 2.
Cart count reached 15 during Session 2 continuation.
